issues.title: [TEST-B] Independent sub-issue B (no Dependencies field)

issues.description:
## Purpose
Synthetic test fixture for DEV-83 integration tests. Do NOT use for real feature work.

This issue exists solely to validate two edge cases in the `autonomous-test-fix-loop` skill:
1. **Cap-enforcement test** — that cycle 4 triggers blocked + escalation with blocked_reason
2. **Unreachable-URL guard** — that missing/5xx deploy_url blocks without creating a fix sub-issue

## Input
- Synthetic feature issue (this issue) with controlled metadata
- No real deploy_url (Test A) / stale URL (Test B)
- test_retry_count pre-seeded to 3 for cap test

## Expected Output
- Test A: issue status → blocked, no child fix sub-issue created
- Test B: issue status → blocked, blocked_reason pinned, escalation comment present, test_retry_count on THIS issue (not a sub-issue)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test A (URL guard) PASS: blocked, no sub-issue created
- [ ] Test B (cap enforcement) PASS: blocked + blocked_reason pinned + escalation comment

## Dependencies
DEV-83

## Risk
Low — synthetic fixture only. Will be cancelled after tests complete.

## Definition of Done
Both tests pass. Results posted on DEV-83.
issues.title: QA FIXTURE: autonomous-test-fix-loop integration test — cap + URL guard

issues.description:
## Owner Squad
Research Intelligence Squad

## Owner Agent
product-manager

## Priority
medium

## Input
Inventory decision: IMPORT microsoft/markitdown (https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown). 120k stars, MIT license, active (April 2026). Converts PDF, Word, Excel, PPT, images → Markdown for LLM consumption. Local file access only.

## Expected Output
- Markitdown MCP server configured for: `product-manager`, `research-scout`, `github-analyst`, `docs-analyst`
- Path allowlist restricted to intake folder (e.g. /uploads, /client-docs)
- Verification: agent can convert a PDF brief to Markdown

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] MCP config updated for target agents
- [ ] Path allowlist configured — only intake/upload directories, not system paths
- [ ] Smoke test: convert a PDF or DOCX file to Markdown from within an agent run
- [ ] Env var / config documented in workspace standards

## Dependencies
- No external credentials required (local processing only)
- Inventory issue DEV-85

## Risk
LOW — local processing. Residual risk: malformed files; mitigate by validating file source before parsing.

## Definition of Done
`product-manager` can parse client PDFs and Word docs into structured Markdown during intake, reducing manual transcription.
issues.title: WIRE: microsoft/markitdown → product-manager + research-scout + github-analyst

issues.description:
## Owner Squad
Architecture Squad

## Owner Agent
integration-architect

## Priority
high

## Input
Inventory decision: IMPORT stripe/agent-toolkit (https://github.com/stripe/agent-toolkit). 966 stars, MIT license, active (official Stripe team). Covers customers, subscriptions, invoices, refunds, payment intents, disputes. Remote MCP available at mcp.stripe.com.

## Expected Output
- Stripe MCP server configured for: `integration-architect`, `backend-engineer`
- Restricted API key (not full secret key) stored in env vars, scoped to: customers:read/write, subscriptions:read/write, payment_intents:write, invoices:read
- Sandbox key for dev; prod key requires explicit workspace owner approval before any wiring
- Verification: agent can create a test customer and a test subscription in sandbox

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] MCP config updated for target agents
- [ ] Restricted Stripe API key (not full secret) provisioned and documented
- [ ] Sandbox key in use for dev; production key NOT wired without explicit approval
- [ ] Minimum required scopes documented in workspace standards
- [ ] Smoke test in sandbox: create customer + subscription

## Dependencies
- Stripe restricted API key requires workspace owner (admin@newtechkw.com)
- Inventory issue DEV-85

## Risk
HIGH — financial transactions. Mitigation: restricted key with minimal scopes; sandbox for all dev/testing; prod requires explicit authorization.

## Definition of Done
`integration-architect` and `backend-engineer` can scaffold Stripe payment flows in new apps using MCP tools.
issues.title: WIRE: stripe/agent-toolkit → integration-architect + backend-engineer

issues.description:
## Owner Squad
QA Security Squad

## Owner Agent
qa-engineer

## Priority
high

## Input
Inventory decision: IMPORT microsoft/playwright-mcp (https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp). 32.7k stars, Apache-2.0 license, active (May 2026). Browser automation — navigate, click, fill forms, screenshot, extract content. 70+ tools. Built into GitHub Copilot.

## Expected Output
- Playwright MCP server configured for: `qa-engineer`, `bug-hunter`
- Chromium dependency installed in agent runtime
- Verification: agent can navigate to a URL, take a screenshot, and fill a form

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] MCP config updated for target agents
- [ ] Playwright/Chromium available in agent runtime environment
- [ ] Agents scoped to allowed URL list (no arbitrary external navigation without oversight)
- [ ] Smoke test: navigate + screenshot from within agent run

## Dependencies
- Chromium installation in runtime environment
- Inventory issue DEV-85

## Risk
MEDIUM — can interact with any web page. Mitigation: run in isolated container; define allowed URL patterns in config.

## Definition of Done
`qa-engineer` can run automated browser tests on deployed apps using Playwright MCP tools.
issues.title: WIRE: microsoft/playwright-mcp → qa-engineer + bug-hunter

issues.description:
## Owner Squad
Architecture Squad

## Owner Agent
database-architect

## Priority
high

## Input
Inventory decision: IMPORT supabase-community/supabase-mcp (https://github.com/supabase-community/supabase-mcp). 2.6k stars, MIT license, 22 commits last 30 days. Full Supabase management: SQL execution, schema, RLS, migrations, logs, Edge Functions.

## Expected Output
- Supabase MCP server configured for: `database-architect` (full admin on dev project), `backend-engineer` (full admin on dev project), `qa-engineer` (read-only mode)
- Service role key stored in env vars (dev project only — never production)
- Verification: agent can execute a SQL query, inspect schema, and run a migration dry-run

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] MCP config updated for target agents with correct access level
- [ ] `--read-only` mode enforced for qa-engineer config
- [ ] Dev project Supabase key in env vars; prod key NOT wired
- [ ] Env var names documented in workspace standards

## Dependencies
- Supabase service role key requires workspace owner (admin@newtechkw.com)
- Inventory issue DEV-85

## Risk
HIGH — full DB admin. Mitigation: dev project only; never wire prod service key; qa-engineer uses read-only mode only.

## Definition of Done
`database-architect` and `backend-engineer` can manage Supabase schema via MCP. `qa-engineer` has read-only access confirmed.
issues.title: WIRE: supabase-community/supabase-mcp → database-architect + backend-engineer

issues.description:
## Owner Squad
Build Squad

## Owner Agent
claude-code-lead-developer

## Priority
high

## Input
Inventory decision: IMPORT github/github-mcp-server (https://github.com/github/github-mcp-server). 54k stars, MIT license, active (May 2026). Full GitHub API access — repos, PRs, issues, branches, CI/CD, file operations.

## Expected Output
- GitHub MCP server configured in the MCP config of: `claude-code-lead-developer`, `backend-engineer`, `qa-engineer`
- A fine-grained PAT scoped to specific repos (not organization-wide) is documented in env vars
- Verification: agent can list repos, open a PR, and read a file via the MCP tool

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] MCP config updated for target agents
- [ ] Fine-grained PAT created with documented minimum scopes (repo read/write, issues, pull_requests, contents — no admin/delete)
- [ ] Env var name documented in workspace standards
- [ ] At least one smoke-test run confirming tool availability

## Dependencies
- GitHub PAT requires workspace owner (admin@newtechkw.com) to provision or approve
- Inventory issue DEV-85 (parent research)

## Risk
HIGH — repo write access. Mitigation: fine-grained PAT scoped per-repo; never use a classic token with wildcard scope.

## Definition of Done
Agents can invoke github-mcp-server tools in their next run. Env var and scope are documented in 00 - Factory Brain.
issues.title: WIRE: github/github-mcp-server → claude-code-lead-developer + backend-engineer

issues.description:
# MCP Server Inventory — App Factory Top-20
**Researcher:** github-analyst | **Date:** 2026-05-26 | **Issue:** DEV-61

## Research Queries Used
1. `modelcontextprotocol MCP servers GitHub 2025 2026 top integrations list`
2. `site:github.com "mcp-server" OR "modelcontextprotocol" database API integration stars`
3. `awesome-mcp-servers GitHub list production integrations 2025`
4. `MCP server GitHub stars 2025 postgres supabase stripe slack notion github filesystem puppeteer`
5. `best-of-mcp-servers ranked list 2026 license activity`

## Filter Applied
- Stars > 500 OR active commit in last 90 days
- License: MIT, Apache-2.0, or BSD (AGPL and Unlicensed excluded)
- Relevance to App Factory verticals: CRM, HR, delivery, inventory, booking, SaaS, payments

## Shortlisted 30 → Narrowed to 20

---

## Top-20 Inventory

### 1. github/github-mcp-server
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/github/github-mcp-server |
| **Purpose** | Full GitHub API — repos, PRs, issues, branches, CI/CD, file operations |
| **Stars / Activity** | 54k stars · Active (May 2026) |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | GitHub token (repo read/write, issues, pull_requests, workflows, contents) |
| **Security Risk** | HIGH — full repo write; mitigate with fine-grained PAT scoped to specific repos only |
| **Recommended Agents** | `claude-code-lead-developer`, `backend-engineer`, `qa-engineer` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — foundational for all build agents to manage code, open PRs, trigger CI |

---

### 2. microsoft/playwright-mcp
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp |
| **Purpose** | Browser automation — navigate, click, fill forms, screenshot, extract content (70+ tools) |
| **Stars / Activity** | 32.7k stars · Active (May 2026) |
| **License** | Apache-2.0 |
| **Perms Requested** | Local Chromium access only; no credentials stored |
| **Security Risk** | MEDIUM — can interact with any site; run in isolated container; never expose to untrusted URLs |
| **Recommended Agents** | `qa-engineer`, `bug-hunter`, `claude-code-lead-developer` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — critical for E2E QA automation and UI testing on every shipped app |

---

### 3. supabase-community/supabase-mcp
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/supabase-community/supabase-mcp |
| **Purpose** | Full Supabase management — SQL execution, schema, RLS, migrations, logs, Edge Functions |
| **Stars / Activity** | 2.6k stars · Active (22 commits in last 30 days, May 2026) |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | Supabase service role key (full admin) or read-only user |
| **Security Risk** | HIGH — full DB admin access; use `--read-only` mode for QA agents; never connect to prod with write access |
| **Recommended Agents** | `database-architect`, `backend-engineer`, `qa-engineer` (read-only) |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — our primary backend platform; essential for schema management and migrations |

---

### 4. stripe/agent-toolkit
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/stripe/agent-toolkit |
| **Purpose** | Stripe payments — customers, subscriptions, invoices, refunds, payment intents, disputes |
| **Stars / Activity** | 966 stars · Active (official Stripe team) |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | Stripe API key (restricted or full; remote MCP at mcp.stripe.com) |
| **Security Risk** | HIGH — financial transactions; use restricted key with only required permissions; sandbox for dev |
| **Recommended Agents** | `integration-architect`, `backend-engineer`, `full-stack-engineer` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — required for every SaaS/booking/subscription app we build |

---

### 5. modelcontextprotocol/servers — filesystem
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/filesystem |
| **Purpose** | Secure file read/write/list with configurable path allowlisting |
| **Stars / Activity** | Part of 79k-star main repo · Active (steering group maintained) |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | Filesystem paths (explicit allowlist via config — no implicit access) |
| **Security Risk** | LOW when paths are restricted; HIGH if misconfigured with broad paths like `/` |
| **Recommended Agents** | All build agents (`claude-code-lead-developer`, `backend-engineer`, `frontend-engineer`) |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — baseline file I/O capability for all coding agents |

---

### 6. modelcontextprotocol/servers — fetch
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/fetch |
| **Purpose** | Fetch web pages and convert HTML → Markdown for LLM consumption |
| **Stars / Activity** | Part of 79k-star main repo · Active |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | Outbound HTTP; no credentials stored |
| **Security Risk** | LOW — outbound only; SSRF risk if user-supplied URLs are passed without validation |
| **Recommended Agents** | `research-scout`, `github-analyst`, `docs-analyst`, `qa-engineer` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — essential for research and doc-reading workflows |

---

### 7. modelcontextprotocol/servers — memory
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/memory |
| **Purpose** | Knowledge graph persistent memory — store/retrieve entities and relations across sessions |
| **Stars / Activity** | Part of 79k-star main repo · Active |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | Local file system (knowledge graph JSON) |
| **Security Risk** | LOW — local only; any sensitive data written to graph persists on disk |
| **Recommended Agents** | `ceo`, `sprint-commander`, `research-scout`, `memory-curator` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — foundational for long-horizon agent memory; supports orchestration quality |

---

### 8. modelcontextprotocol/servers — git
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/git |
| **Purpose** | Local git operations — diff, log, commit, branch, blame, search history |
| **Stars / Activity** | Part of 79k-star main repo · Active |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | Local git repo paths |
| **Security Risk** | LOW — read-heavy; write ops require explicit allowance |
| **Recommended Agents** | `claude-code-lead-developer`, `backend-engineer`, `frontend-engineer` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — essential for local repo operations during build workflow |

---

### 9. modelcontextprotocol/servers — sequential-thinking
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/sequentialthinking |
| **Purpose** | Dynamic structured reasoning — breaks complex tasks into verifiable thought steps |
| **Stars / Activity** | Part of 79k-star main repo · Active |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | None (pure reasoning; no external API) |
| **Security Risk** | NONE |
| **Recommended Agents** | `ceo`, `sprint-commander`, `solution-architect`, `database-architect` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — improves planning accuracy for complex multi-step tasks |

---

### 10. awslabs/mcp
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/awslabs/mcp |
| **Purpose** | AWS services suite — documentation, CDK IaC, Lambda, S3, Cost Analysis, Support, Knowledge |
| **Stars / Activity** | 9.1k stars · Active (May 2026) |
| **License** | Apache-2.0 |
| **Perms Requested** | AWS credentials (IAM role — scope per sub-server used) |
| **Security Risk** | MEDIUM-HIGH — cloud infra write access; use IAM roles with least-privilege per use case |
| **Recommended Agents** | `deployment-engineer`, `environment-manager`, `solution-architect` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — needed for AWS-hosted deployments and IaC-driven infrastructure |

---

### 11. cloudflare/mcp-server-cloudflare
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/cloudflare/mcp-server-cloudflare |
| **Purpose** | Cloudflare Workers, KV, R2, D1, Pages — edge deployment, storage, CDN management |
| **Stars / Activity** | 3.2k stars · Active |
| **License** | Apache-2.0 |
| **Perms Requested** | Cloudflare API token (account-scoped; restrict to specific services) |
| **Security Risk** | MEDIUM — infra write access; scope token to only the services needed |
| **Recommended Agents** | `deployment-engineer`, `environment-manager` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — preferred edge deployment layer for SaaS apps; Cloudflare D1/R2 used in stack |

---

### 12. makenotion/notion-mcp-server
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/makenotion/notion-mcp-server |
| **Purpose** | Notion CRUD — pages, databases, comments, search; also remote MCP via OAuth |
| **Stars / Activity** | 3.7k stars · Active (v2.0 with Notion API 2025-09-03) |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | Notion integration token (workspace-scoped; restrict to specific pages/DBs) |
| **Security Risk** | LOW-MEDIUM — knowledge base read/write; restrict integration to non-sensitive pages |
| **Recommended Agents** | `product-manager`, `sprint-commander`, `documentation-agent`, `skill-builder` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — PRD drafting, knowledge management, and team documentation |

---

### 13. korotovsky/slack-mcp-server
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/korotovsky/slack-mcp-server |
| **Purpose** | Slack — read channels/DMs, send messages, search history, group DMs |
| **Stars / Activity** | 1.4k stars · Active |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | Slack bot token (channels:read, chat:write, users:read; no workspace-wide admin) |
| **Security Risk** | LOW-MEDIUM — message content exposure; scope bot to specific channels |
| **Recommended Agents** | `ceo`, `sprint-commander`, `workspace-operations-manager` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — agent-to-human notification channel for approvals and alerts |

---

### 14. zapier/zapier-mcp
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/zapier/zapier-mcp |
| **Purpose** | 9,000+ app integrations as callable tools via Zapier; covers gaps with no native MCP |
| **Stars / Activity** | Remote-first; official by Zapier · Active |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | Zapier OAuth; scope depends on which Zaps are exposed |
| **Security Risk** | MEDIUM — depends on Zap config; audit each exposed Zap before wiring |
| **Recommended Agents** | `sprint-commander`, `integration-architect`, `full-stack-engineer` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — bridge for integrations lacking native MCP (WhatsApp, CRM connectors, etc.) |

---

### 15. googleapis/genai-toolbox
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/googleapis/genai-toolbox |
| **Purpose** | Google Cloud databases — BigQuery, Cloud SQL (Postgres/MySQL), Spanner, AlloyDB, Bigtable |
| **Stars / Activity** | 15k stars · Active (May 2026) |
| **License** | Apache-2.0 |
| **Perms Requested** | GCP service account (IAM-scoped per database) |
| **Security Risk** | MEDIUM — data warehouse access; use read-only service accounts for analytical queries |
| **Recommended Agents** | `database-architect`, `analytics-lead`, `backend-engineer` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — needed for analytics/BI layers in apps requiring BigQuery or Cloud SQL |

---

### 16. microsoft/markitdown
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/microsoft/markitdown |
| **Purpose** | Convert PDF, Word, Excel, PPT, images → Markdown for LLM ingestion |
| **Stars / Activity** | 120k stars · Active (April 2026) |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | Local file access only (reads from configured paths) |
| **Security Risk** | LOW — local processing; malformed files could exploit parser; validate source files |
| **Recommended Agents** | `product-manager`, `research-scout`, `github-analyst`, `docs-analyst` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — critical for parsing client specs, RFPs, and existing system docs during intake |

---

### 17. meilisearch/meilisearch-mcp
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/meilisearch/meilisearch-mcp |
| **Purpose** | Full-text + semantic search — index management, document ops, search, settings |
| **Stars / Activity** | 160 stars · Active (official by Meilisearch) |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | Meilisearch API key (master key for admin; search-only key for queries) |
| **Security Risk** | LOW — use search-only key in production; master key dev-only |
| **Recommended Agents** | `backend-engineer`, `full-stack-engineer` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — used in every app with a search bar (inventory, CRM, delivery, booking) |

---

### 18. axiomhq/mcp-server-axiom
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/axiomhq/mcp-server-axiom |
| **Purpose** | Query logs, events, traces with natural language → Axiom Processing Language (APL) |
| **Stars / Activity** | Official by Axiom · Active |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | Axiom API token (dataset-scoped; read-only sufficient) |
| **Security Risk** | LOW — read-only log data |
| **Recommended Agents** | `qa-engineer`, `security-auditor`, `deployment-engineer`, `performance-tester` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — production observability for all deployed apps; enables AI-driven log triage |

---

### 19. paypal/agent-toolkit
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/paypal/agent-toolkit |
| **Purpose** | PayPal payments — orders, subscriptions, disputes, refunds, webhooks |
| **Stars / Activity** | Official by PayPal · Active |
| **License** | Apache-2.0 |
| **Perms Requested** | PayPal client credentials (OAuth; scope to specific features) |
| **Security Risk** | HIGH — financial transactions; sandbox for dev; restrict prod scopes to minimum needed |
| **Recommended Agents** | `integration-architect`, `backend-engineer` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — GCC/Kuwait markets use PayPal; needed alongside Stripe for regional coverage |

---

### 20. integromat/make-mcp-server
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **GitHub URL** | https://github.com/integromat/make-mcp-server |
| **Purpose** | Trigger Make.com (Integromat) scenarios as callable tools from agents |
| **Stars / Activity** | Official by Integromat · Active |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Perms Requested** | Make API key (scoped to specific scenarios) |
| **Security Risk** | LOW-MEDIUM — depends on what scenarios are exposed; audit before wiring |
| **Recommended Agents** | `sprint-commander`, `integration-architect` |
| **Decision** | **IMPORT** — complements Zapier for clients already on Make; handles complex automation flows |

---

## Rejected Candidates (with Reasons)

| Server | Reason for Rejection |
|---|---|
| Skyvern-AI/skyvern (22k⭐) | AGPL-3.0 — viral copyleft; cannot use in commercial products without open-sourcing |
| mindsdb/mindsdb (39k⭐) | Unlicensed on GitHub — legal risk; no clear commercial use terms |
| tacticlaunch/mcp-linear (96⭐) | Low adoption; App Factory uses Multica for PM, not Linear |
| square/square-mcp-server | US-market only; App Factory targets Kuwait/GCC (use PayPal + KNet instead) |
| bytedance/UI-TARS-desktop (35k⭐) | Desktop app, not MCP server library; mismatched use case |
| pydantic/pydantic-ai (17k⭐) | Framework, not MCP server — evaluated separately as SDK option |
| elie222/inbox-zero (11k⭐) | Unlicensed; email automation better handled via Zapier/Make |
| korotovsky/slack-mcp-server duplicates | Evaluated primary repo only |

---

## Top-5 Priority Imports

| Priority | Server | Assign To | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | github/github-mcp-server | `claude-code-lead-developer` | Every build requires repo management |
| 2 | supabase-community/supabase-mcp | `database-architect` | Core backend for every app |
| 3 | microsoft/playwright-mcp | `qa-engineer` | E2E testing on all shipped apps |
| 4 | stripe/agent-toolkit | `integration-architect` | Required for SaaS/booking payment flows |
| 5 | microsoft/markitdown | `product-manager` | Intake parsing for client docs/RFPs |
issues.title: INVENTORY: Top-20 MCP Servers for App Factory

issues.description:
## Input
Parent App Request: [DEV-25](mention://issue/f01f5264-86df-4f80-a2ff-f6a1abbb0794).
PRD sub-issue (must land first): [DEV-26](mention://issue/799e091f-184e-4551-8f8d-fd55cf62c59f).

Two surfaces:
- **Employee Android app** (React Native + Expo) — primary, mobile-only, RTL-first.
- **HR Admin web dashboard** (Next.js) — desktop primary, tablet supported.

## Expected Output
UX Architect (lead) coordinates UI Designer + Arabic RTL Experience Agent to produce:

1. **Sitemap** for both surfaces — every reachable screen.
2. **Screen list** (`dashboard-screen-planner`) per-screen spec: purpose, key components, primary CTA, secondary actions, data shown, permission gate, empty/loading/error variants. Must apply:
   - `wcag-accessibility-checklist` (WCAG 2.2 AA)
   - `i18n-multilang-setup`
   - `rtl-arabic-ui-planner` (RTL mirroring, Arabic-KW copy, locale-aware date/number)
3. **Mobile screens** (Android, RTL-first):
   - Login (employee ID or magic link), Today (status card, big check-in/out button), Check-in flow (geofence check → selfie → QR option → confirmation), History (30 days), Shift (this week + next), Leave (submit + status), Settings, Offline indicator + queue view.
4. **Admin web screens** (LTR + RTL):
   - Login (MFA optional), Dashboard (live attendance board), Employees (CRUD + photo + civil ID + department + manager + shift), Offices (CRUD + geofence map), Shifts (CRUD + grace + break), Live Attendance, Reports (daily/weekly/monthly, CSV + PDF), Leave Management (approve/reject), Audit Log, Settings (roles, feature flags, tenant config).
5. **Arabic-KW copy plan** (`arabic-rtl-experience-agent`) — every screen's primary labels in Arabic-KW + English. Native, not machine-translated.
6. **Mobile layout** described for any screen at ≤375px.
7. **Role visibility** matrix per screen (Super Admin · HR Manager · Dept Manager · Employee).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every screen has empty/loading/error variants
- [ ] Every Arabic-bound screen has RTL variant noted
- [ ] Mobile layout described at 375px for every mobile screen
- [ ] Role visibility called out per screen
- [ ] Arabic-KW copy is native (reviewed by `arabic-rtl-experience-agent`), not translated
- [ ] Per-screen permission gate matches role matrix from DEV-26

## Dependencies
DEV-26 (PRD + role matrix + user stories). Will be promoted to `todo` by Sprint Commander once DEV-26 lands.

## Risk
- Selfie capture on Android has device-specific camera quirks — note in UX states (camera permission denied, low light, blurred).
- Offline queue view UX — must show pending punches clearly to avoid duplicate taps.

## Definition of Done
Sitemap + screen list + Arabic copy plan + mobile layouts posted as artifacts on this issue. Hand off to Build Squad via comment on parent (DEV-25). Move this issue to `in_review`.

## Routing
Owner: Product UX Squad (ux-architect lead).
issues.title: UX Pack — Sitemap, Screen List, RTL/Arabic Plan — Android Attendance App

issues.description:
## Input
Parent App Request: [DEV-25](mention://issue/f01f5264-86df-4f80-a2ff-f6a1abbb0794).
PRD sub-issue (must land first): [DEV-26](mention://issue/799e091f-184e-4551-8f8d-fd55cf62c59f).

Default stack confirmed per parent: **React Native (Expo) Android** + **Next.js HR admin** + **Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Storage + Realtime + Edge Functions)** + **FCM** for push + **Vercel** for web + **EAS Build** for APK/AAB. Do NOT deviate without CEO approval.

## Expected Output
Solution Architect (lead) coordinates Database Architect + API Architect + Integration Architect to produce the full Architecture pack:

1. **ADR-001** — stack choice, rejected alternatives + reasons, cost note at 100/1k/10k users.
2. **Database schema** (`database-schema-designer`) — Postgres DDL with RLS on every user-scoped table. Must apply:
   - `multi-tenant-architecture` (workspace_id on every table)
   - `audit-log-designer` (every check-in/out + admin edit logged with actor, ip, device)
   - Core tables: tenants, users, employees, departments, offices (with geofence radius), shifts, shift_assignments, punches (check-in/out with location, selfie_url, qr_code, status, offline_synced_at), leave_requests, audit_log, push_tokens, feature_flags, public_holidays.
3. **OpenAPI 3.1 contract** (`api-contract-designer`) — every endpoint, error model, pagination, versioning. Must apply:
   - `api-versioning-strategy` (e.g. `/v1`)
   - `rate-limiting-design` (punch endpoint: 1 per 30s per employee)
4. **Auth model** wired to the role matrix from DEV-26 — Supabase Auth, JWT, RLS policies, refresh strategy, RBAC for 4 roles.
5. **Integrations brief** (`integration-architect`) — FCM (push), Supabase Storage (selfies), optional MyFatoorah (Phase 2 payroll), email fallback (Resend or Supabase SMTP), Sentry, PostHog. Each: purpose, auth, rate limits, failure mode, idempotency, retry policy.
6. **Security model** — secret storage, PII inventory (civil ID, biometric, location), retention, audit log requirement, threat model summary, PDPL-Kuwait classification.
7. **Env vars list** for local/preview/staging/prod.
8. **List of external scaffolds required** — to be escalated to admin@newtechkw.com:
   - Supabase project (free tier OK for MVP)
   - GitHub repo
   - Vercel project
   - Firebase project (FCM)
   - Expo / EAS account

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ADR-001 names rejected alternatives
- [ ] Every user-scoped table has RLS policy in DDL
- [ ] Every API endpoint has an error model
- [ ] Every integration has documented failure mode
- [ ] Secrets management defined; no secret ever in client bundle
- [ ] Punch endpoint rate-limit is concrete (e.g. token bucket, 1/30s)
- [ ] Audit log covers every destructive action
- [ ] Env vars list complete per environment
- [ ] Credentials escalation comment drafted (not yet sent — wait for CEO trigger)

## Dependencies
DEV-26 (PRD + role matrix). Will be promoted to `todo` by Sprint Commander once DEV-26 lands.

## Risk
- Anti-buddy-punch with selfie-only is weak; document the limitation in the security model and flag face-recognition as Phase 2.
- GPS spoofing — geofence + WiFi SSID fingerprint as MVP defense, BLE beacons Phase 2.
- Battery drain — foreground-only location requests at punch time, no background tracking.

## Definition of Done
ADR + schema + OpenAPI + auth model + integrations brief + security model + env list posted as artifacts on this issue. Comment on parent (DEV-25) with handoff summary + credentials list. Then create Build sub-issues in `05 - Parallel Build` via `user-stories-to-issues` (status `backlog`; CEO promotes when credentials land). Move this issue to `in_review`.

## Routing
Owner: Architecture Squad (solution-architect lead).
issues.title: Architecture Pack — Android Attendance App (stack, schema, API, integrations)
